title: Rosa afzeliana f. transiens
what_description: Rosa afzeliana f. transiens is a plant that belongs to the family Rosaceae. It is a deciduous shrub with a height range of 2-4 meters, having a simple, pinnate or palmate leaf arrangement with 3-7 leaflets, and stipules that are triangular or lanceolate in shape. The leaves are 5-10 centimeters long and have a serrated margin, with a dark green color on the upper surface and a lighter green or glaucous color on the lower surface. The flowers are pink to red, with 5 petals, and are arranged in a corymbose or racemose inflorescence, containing 5-15 flowers. The plant has a woody stem with a greyish-brown bark and a dense, thorny habit.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Rosa afzeliana f. transiens are inflammation, which can be reduced by its anti-inflammatory properties, anxiety and stress, alleviated by its anxiolytic and adaptogenic effects, pain and fungal infections, treated by its antifungal and analgesic properties, depression, improved by its antidepressant activity, insomnia, addressed by its sedative action, digestive issues, resolved by its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, diabetes, managed by its antidiabetic activity, hypertension, lowered by its vasodilatory effect, skin irritation, relieved by its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, cancer, inhibited by its antiproliferative and pro-apoptotic effects, arthritis, treated by its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, skin issues, resolved by its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, wounds, accelerated by its antiseptic and wound-healing properties, gastrointestinal issues, improved by its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, high blood pressure, lowered by its vasodilatory effect, skin conditions, alleviated by its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, and insect bites, relieved by its antiseptic and analgesic properties.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Rosa afzeliana f. transiens are its antioxidant activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, these compounds help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Additionally, the plant exhibits antimicrobial properties, particularly against bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of saponins and volatile oils. Furthermore, the anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects of Rosa afzeliana f. transiens are thought to be mediated by the bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and neurotransmitters. The antifungal properties of this plant are also noteworthy, as they are attributed to the presence of volatile oils, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungal species. These bioactive compounds and medicinal properties make Rosa afzeliana f. transiens a valuable resource for the development of new therapeutic agents.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Rosa afzeliana f. transiens are quercetin, a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory flavonoid; rosmarinic acid, a diterpene with significant antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties; kaempferol, a flavonol with strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects; gallic acid, a polyphenol with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties; and epicatechin, a flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. These constituents contribute to the plant's therapeutic potential by mitigating oxidative stress, inflammation, and microbial infections. Quercetin and kaempferol exhibit anti-inflammatory activity by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, while rosmarinic acid and gallic acid display antimicrobial activity by disrupting microbial membranes and inhibiting microbial growth. Epicatechin also contributes to the plant's antioxidant capacity by scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress. The combination of these constituents in Rosa afzeliana f. transiens may provide a synergistic effect, enhancing its potential as a natural therapeutic agent for various health conditions.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Rosa afzeliana f. transiens are rosewater, rose oil, rose tea, rose syrup, and rosehip tincture. Rosewater is a hydrosol produced through steam distillation of rose petals, often used in skincare and perfumery due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Rose oil is extracted through solvent or steam distillation, primarily used in aromatherapy and perfumery to alleviate stress and promote relaxation. Rose tea is a herbal infusion made by steeping dried rose petals in hot water, traditionally consumed to aid digestive issues and promote sleep. Rosehip tincture is a concentrated extract of rose hips, rich in vitamins A and C, used to support immune function and skin health due to its antioxidant properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Rosa afzeliana f. transiens improperly are experiences of allergic reactions, which can range from mild hives to life-threatening anaphylaxis, due to its high concentration of allergenic compounds like mal d 1. Developing skin irritation is also a potential side effect, as its sap can cause contact dermatitis and skin redness in some individuals. Additionally, Rosa afzeliana f. transiens may increase blood pressure in people who are sensitive to its flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, which can exacerbate hypertension. Furthermore, its high tannin content may also cause stomach upset and digestive issues in individuals who consume it in large quantities. Improper use of Rosa afzeliana f. transiens may also lead to the development of skin irritation and experience of allergic reactions in some individuals.
